content:
Member-only story

4 min read
Just now

--

Every aspiring founder asks the same question before building their first product:
"What's the best tech stack?"
I used to spend days comparing frameworks, databases, authentication providers, and deployment platforms. By the time I finally made a decision, someone else had already launched their MVP.
Eventually, I realized something important.
The best tech stack isn't the one with the most GitHub stars.
It's the one that helps you ship.
Today, AI has completely changed how we build software. A solo developer can go from an idea to a live product in a weekend without spending hundreds of dollars on infrastructure.
This is the stack I personally recommend if your goal is simple:
Build fast. Launch fast. Learn fast.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Myrevlink.inStep 1: Start with ChatGPT, Not Your Code Editor
Most failed startups don't fail because of bad code.
They fail because they solve problems nobody actually has.
Before writing a single line of code, I spend time with ChatGPT.
I use it to:
